personas.name: Private Emotional Support Seeker
personas.description: A user who turns to Wysa as a safe, companion-like space to vent, feel heard, and receive emotional support without fear of judgment.
goals: Feel emotionally supported during difficult moments
Use the app as a comforting companion when human support is unavailable
pain_points: May not have someone available to talk to right away
context: Uses the app alone during emotionally difficult moments
Often relies on the app when immediate human support is unavailable
constraints: The app must maintain a supportive and non-judgmental tone
Interactions should feel private and emotionally safe
personas.notes: I changed from the automated persona to remove vague wording and align more closely with the companion-like support theme in H1.

personas.name: Anxiety and Stress Relief User
personas.description: A user who relies on Wysa to reduce anxiety, calm stress, manage overthinking, and access coping support during difficult moments.
goals: Feel calmer and more in control of thoughts
pain_points: Experiences anxiety spikes and emotional overwhelm
May struggle with stress, overthinking, or tension
Needs support that is available right away
context: Uses the app during stressful situations or anxious episodes
Often opens the app for quick self-help support
constraints: Support should be quick to access
Coping tools should be understandable during distress
personas.notes: I kept the core automated idea but made the persona more specific and traceable to anxiety.

personas.name: Grateful User Who Values Accessibility
personas.description: A user who values Wysa because it feels helpful, accessible, lightweight, and easy to turn to during moments of need.
goals: Use a helpful mental-health support tool with low friction
Continue using an app that feels worthwhile and approachable
pain_points: Needs support that feels available and usable without much setup
May be discouraged by tools that feel heavy or complicated
Wants continued value from the app experience
context: Uses the app as an easy-to-access support tool
Appreciates simple access and the sense that the app is genuinely helpful
constraints: The app should remain lightweight and accessible
The user experience should not add unnecessary barriers
personas.notes: I refined from a generic positive persona into one grounded in appreciation, and accessibility.

personas.name: User Frustrated by AI Quality, Support, or Pricing
personas.description: A user who becomes dissatisfied when Wysa feels unresponsive, low-quality, overpriced, or unsupported by helpful customer service.
goals: Receive useful and coherent support from the app
Get fair value for paid features
Be able to report issues and receive meaningful help
pain_points: Encounters weak or irrelevant AI responses
Feels pricing may be too high for the value received
May struggle to get useful customer support
context: Uses the app long enough to notice AI limitations or pricing frustration
May compare actual app value against expectations for paid mental-health support
constraints: Responses should feel functional and relevant
Pricing and support experience should feel justified and transparent
personas.notes: I tried to combine the automated dissatisfaction cluster into a grounded persona focused on AI quality, support responsiveness, and pricing concerns.

personas.name: User Experiencing Recovery and Emotional Improvement
personas.description: A user who uses Wysa as part of a broader mental-health recovery journey and values the app when it helps improve mood, coping, and emotional stability.
goals: Use the app as a helpful part of ongoing coping and recovery
pain_points: May be recovering from serious emotional distress or depression
Needs support that feels meaningful over time
Wants progress and improvement rather than temporary distraction
context: Uses the app repeatedly during a recovery journey
Sees the app as one source of support for improving mental well-being
constraints: Support should feel consistent and genuinely helpful
The app should fit into longer-term emotional improvement and recovery use
personas.notes: I changed the automated recovery persona into one explicitly grounded in H5’s recovery and mental-health improvement evidence.
